channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech

item.title: Vector Institute aims to clear up confusion about AI model performance
item.description: AI models are advancing at a dizzying clip, with builders boasting ever more impressive performance with each iteration.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Google unveils Firebase Studio for AI app development
item.description: Google is previewing Firebase Studio, a cloud-based agentic development environment designed to build, test, deploy, and run AI applications.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Here's an Apple Studio Display alternative for the rest of us - the oddly-named Dough Spectrum Canvas offers 6K resolution
item.description: Dough Spectrum Canvas delivers a 6K experience for Mac users, offering a high-quality alternative to Apple's expensive Studio Display.
item.dc:creator: TechRadar

item.title: Hackers breach Morocco's social security database in an unprecedented cyberattack
item.description: RABAT, Morocco (AP) — Morocco 's social security agency said troves of data were stolen from its systems in a cyberattack this week that resulted in personal information being leaked on the messaging app Telegram.
item.dc:creator: SAM METZ, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Alphabet can’t shake off AI concerns even with low multiple
item.description: Persistent concerns that Alphabet Inc. has fallen behind in the artificial intelligence supremacy race has weighed on the stock this year, and not even the cheapest earnings multiple in about two years, or limited risk from tariffs, has been enough to entice investors.
item.dc:creator: Bloomberg, The Mercury News

item.title: OpenAI countersues Elon Musk in legal dispute over ChatGPT maker's business ambitions
item.description: OpenAI is suing Elon Musk for unfair competition and interfering with its business relationships with investors and customers, escalating a legal battle between the ChatGPT maker and the billionaire who helped bankroll the artificial intelligence startup a decade ago.
item.dc:creator: MATT O'BRIEN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Google reaffirms $75B AI infra investment as cloud providers pursue divergent strategies
item.description: In a move closely watched by enterprise technology leaders, Alphabet CEO Sundar Pichai has reaffirmed Google’s commitment to spending $75 billion this year on AI infrastructure and data centers — weeks after Microsoft reportedly abandoned many of its data center projects.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: One Tech Tip: Locking down your device when crossing borders
item.description: LONDON (AP) — Planning an international trip? Travelers should prepare for the possibility of extra scrutiny of their phones when crossing borders, especially when entering the United States. The Canadian government warned travelers in a recent travel advisory that U.S.
item.dc:creator: KELVIN CHAN, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: IPVanish Confirms No Data Logging with Latest Audit and Transparency Report
item.description: An independent audit confirms IPVanish doesn't store user data, while a new transparency report details government requests and malicious activity attempts.
item.dc:creator: chiara.castro@futurenet.com (Chiara Castro), TechRadar

item.title: Microsoft says it's 'slowing or pausing' some AI data center projects, including $1B plan for Ohio
item.description: Microsoft said it is “slowing or pausing” some of its data center construction, including a $1 billion project in Ohio, the latest sign that the demand for artificial intelligence technology that drove a massive infrastructure expansion might not need quite as many powerful computers as expected.
item.dc:creator: MATT O'BRIEN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Scientists map part of a mouse's brain that's so complex it looks like a galaxy
item.description: Thanks to a mouse watching clips from “The Matrix,” scientists have created the largest functional map of a brain to date.
item.dc:creator: Lauran Neergaard, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Top network and data center events 2025
item.description: Ready to travel to gain hands-on experience with new networking and infrastructure tools? Tech conferences – in person and virtual – give attendees a chance to access product demos, network with peers, earn continuing education credits, and catch a celebrity keynote or live entertainment.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: 200 million social media records leaked in major X data breach
item.description: X hit with massive data breach with 200 million records leaked, including emails. Tech expert Kurt “CyberGuy" Knutsson offers seven tips to help protect yourself.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: UALink releases inaugural GPU interconnect specification
item.description: The multivendor Ultra Accelerator Link (UALink) consortium has published its first specification aimed at delivering an open standard interconnect for AI clusters – and providing an alternative to Nvidia’s proprietary NVLink.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: A wild new way to think about Android apps
item.description: Fair warning: This column is a fair bit different from the type of thinking we typically take on in these musty virtual quarters of ours.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: What is Kubernetes? Scalable cloud-native applications
item.description: Kubernetes is a popular open source platform for container orchestration—that is, for managing applications built from multiple, largely self-contained runtimes called containers.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Five things to consider before you deploy an LLM
item.description: If the screwdriver were invented by the tech industry today, then it would be widely deployed for a variety of tasks, including hammering nails. Since the debut of ChatGPT, there has been growing fervor and backlash against large language models (LLMs).
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Four paradoxes of software development
item.description: Civil engineers can rightfully claim that no two bridges are exactly alike. However, bridges share many known characteristics, and the materials they are built with have known characteristics. Building bridges involves many known knowns and not as many unknown unknowns as one might think.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Scientists genetically engineer wolves with white hair and muscular jaws like the extinct dire wolf
item.description: Three genetically engineered wolves that may resemble extinct dire wolves are trotting, sleeping and howling in an undisclosed secure location in the U.S., according to the company that aims to bring back lost species.
item.dc:creator: CHRISTINA LARSON, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Microsoft workers say they've been fired after 50th anniversary protest over Israel contract
item.description: Microsoft has fired two employees who interrupted the company's 50th anniversary celebration to protest its work supplying artificial intelligence technology to the Israeli military, according to a group representing the workers.
item.dc:creator: MATT O'BRIEN, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: A cyber call to arms
item.description: Without a call to arms, the United States is dangerously vulnerable today, and still more tomorrow, to increasingly sophisticated and brazen cyberattacks. The post A cyber call to arms first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: commentators, Federal News Network

item.title: AI vs. pro gambler's $1 million March Madness bet coming down to Duke and Houston
item.description: SAN ANTONIO (AP) — The $1 million March Madness wager between a pro gambler and an artificial-intelligence site will be decided by the winner of the Duke-Houston game in the Final Four. The Vegas bookies project the gambler, who picked 5 1/2-point favorite Duke, should win.
item.dc:creator: EDDIE PELLS, Associated Press, KREX 5
